{{Quote|My memories tell me that, for a time, Bochaba was one of the most feared gangsters on Sleheyron.|HK-47|<ref name="KOTORg" />}}
'''Bochaba''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sleheyron/Legends|Sleheyron]] around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Jedi Civil War]]. He gained notoriety when he took possession of an [[HK-series assassin droid|HK-series]] [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] with the designation [[HK-47]]. Bochaba installed several programs to improve the performance of the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] and used its [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] protocols to [[Death/Legends|kill]] three hundred twenty-two [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] beings, the Hutt's competitors, in a single [[Standard year/Legends|year]]. These successful eliminations made Bochaba very wealthy.<ref name="KOTORg" />

However, the [[Exchange]] crime syndicate opposed Bochaba and had the Hutt killed in a grizzly fashion in retaliation. As HK-47 would later explain to [[Revan/Legends|one of his later owners]], the residents of the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] were "fishing pieces of Bochaba out of their soup for weeks afterward." Upon discovering his master's death, HK-47 shut down and a [[Unidentified Hutt (Bochaba's rival)|rival Hutt]] claimed him as his own, later selling the droid to a [[Unidentified Human senator|Senator]].<ref name="KOTORg" />

==Behind the scenes==
Bochaba was introduced in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]], ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', as part of HK-47's backstory, which can be discovered by the player through conversations with HK-47.
